title: Dryopteris mollis
taxonomy: Dryopteris mollis is classified as a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This family of ferns is part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. The genus Dryopteris is a large and diverse group of ferns, commonly known as wood ferns. Dryopteris mollis is native to Europe and North Africa, and is often found growing in woodland areas. The species is characterized by its soft, leathery fronds and is often used as an ornamental plant in gardens.

In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant properties. intro: Dryopteris mollis, also known as soft shield fern or lady fern, is a type of plant that belongs to the fern family (Dryopteridaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to support immune function and protect against cell damage.
